$schema: This is descriptive research evidence, not normative doctrine
note: Consolidated sources from cinema color research document. These represent the intellectual landscape of color discourse in cinema.

sources.title: Writings on Apocalypse Now
sources.author: Vittorio Storaro
sources.description: Storaro's primary source material discussing color as physiological force in Apocalypse Now

sources.title: Writings on The Last Emperor
sources.author: Vittorio Storaro
sources.description: Storaro discusses color wavelengths physically altering viewer's metabolism

sources.title: Writing with Light
sources.author: Vittorio Storaro
sources.description: Storaro's conceptual framework for cinematography as literary function

sources.title: Interviews on working with Wong Kar-wai
sources.author: Christopher Doyle
sources.description: Reveals reactive, improvisational approach to color. Discusses In the Mood for Love

sources.title: Defense of green cast in Days of Being Wild
sources.author: Christopher Doyle
sources.description: Critical text for understanding 'Atmospheric Color' - green as faithful rendering of 1960s Hong Kong humidity and fluorescent reality

sources.title: Interviews regarding Blade Runner 2049
sources.author: Roger Deakins
sources.description: Counter-argument to aggressive digital grading. Discusses justified stylization

sources.title: Interviews regarding No Country for Old Men
sources.author: Roger Deakins
sources.description: Discusses naturalistic stylization approach

sources.title: Skepticism of HDR (High Dynamic Range)
sources.author: Roger Deakins
sources.description: Suggests philosophical preference for controlled contrast over 'pop'

sources.title: The Politics of Underexposure
sources.author: Bradford Young
sources.description: Essential discourse on Cultural Context. Challenges technical bias calibrated for white skin

sources.title: Interview on Carol
sources.author: Edward Lachman
sources.description: Blueprint for 'Media-Referential' color. Discusses Super 16mm to emulate Ektachrome

sources.title: Sight & Sound
sources.description: Critical theory source. Provides interpretive layer for how technical choices are received by cultural consciousness

sources.title: MUBI Notebook
sources.description: Critical theory source. Essay film and visual analysis. Discusses Touki Bouki and Red Desert as post-colonial and modernist color use

sources.title: Criterion Collection Essays
sources.description: Critical theory source. Interpretive layer for technical choices

sources.title: The Myth of Total Cinema
sources.author: André Bazin
sources.description: Argues cinema strives for 'total representation of reality.' Complicates understanding of color as spectacle vs. realism

sources.title: The Teal & Orange Discourse
sources.author: Multiple critics
sources.description: Cluster of articles treating homogenization of blockbusters into Teal/Orange paradigm as 'scourge'

sources.title: Memory Color Effect (MCE) Research
sources.author: Various researchers
sources.description: Research indicating human memory is 'color diagnostic.' We remember objects as hyper-real ideal

sources.title: Color & Emotional Retention Studies
sources.author: Various researchers
sources.description: Studies show warm colors elicit positive evaluations, but cool colors in specific contexts (like Moonlight) signal intimacy

sources.title: Three-Strip Technicolor Process Analysis
sources.author: Film historians
sources.description: Analysis of Jack Cardiff's work on Black Narcissus and The Red Shoes. Documents technical requirements and aesthetic implications

sources.title: Eastman Kodak 5247 Stock Analysis
sources.author: Film historians
sources.description: Analysis of 1970s film stocks that could be 'pushed' in development, enabling New Hollywood grit aesthetic

sources.title: Silver Retention (ENR) Process Documentation
sources.author: Technical sources
sources.description: Khondji's bleach bypass technique. Leaving silver in print creates dense blacks and desaturated, metallic colors

sources.title: Analysis of Red Desert Color Methodology
sources.author: Film scholars
sources.description: Documents Antonioni physically painting grass, fruit, and streets grey for psychological projection

sources.title: Kieślowski on Three Colors: Blue
sources.author: Krzysztof Kieślowski
sources.description: Director clarifies colors are atmospheric, not rigidly symbolic

sources.title: Analysis of Inverted Color Meaning in Moonlight
sources.author: Film critics
sources.description: Documents blue as warmth and intimacy, counter to traditional color psychology

sources.title: Analysis of Inverted Horror Aesthetics in Midsommar
sources.author: Film critics
sources.description: Documents weaponization of happy palette to create cognitive dissonance

sources.title: Benjamin Loeb on Mandy Lighting Effects
sources.author: Benjamin Loeb
sources.description: Discusses practical lighting effects and texture creation

sources.title: Analysis of Color as Perspective in The Florida Project
sources.author: Film critics
sources.description: Documents candy colors reflecting child's perspective, creating tragic dissonance

sources.title: Analysis of Color as Political Symbolism in Timbuktu
sources.author: Film critics
sources.description: Documents vibrant color as resistance against monochromatic authoritarianism

sources.title: Analysis of Hitchcock's Use of Green in Vertigo
sources.author: Film scholars
sources.description: Documents green as uncanny, unnatural color of obsession

sources.title: Analysis of Contemporary Neon-Noir Aesthetic
sources.author: Film critics
sources.description: Documents toxic neon palette in Good Time, Spring Breakers as altered consciousness signaling

sources.title: The Rise of Video Essay as Film Criticism
sources.author: Media scholars
sources.description: Highlights video essay as primary mode. Color designs analyzed as standalone texts

sources.title: Essay on In the Mood for Love Color
sources.author: Film critics
sources.description: Describes color as emotionally expressive, tied to desire, repetition, and memory

source_categories.primary_philosophy: Direct testimony from cinematographers - distinguishes style from governing worldview
source_categories.critical_theory: Interpretive layer from Sight & Sound, MUBI Notebook, Criterion - explains cultural reception
source_categories.cognitive_science: Research on how brain processes color in motion - moves beyond subjective adjectives
source_categories.technical_history: Documentation of film stocks, processes, and their aesthetic implications
source_categories.film_analysis: Case studies of specific films and their color methodologies
source_categories.meta_criticism: Analysis of how we analyze - video essays, critical frameworks
